description: Version-pinned and entirely-malicious package IOCs. Shipped with repo-forensics as baseline detection. Optional OSV feed adds live updates (see --osv flag). Use '*' in the version list to mean 'any version of this package is malicious'.

chalk_debug_sep_2025.title: Chalk/Debug supply chain compromise
chalk_debug_sep_2025.notes: Maintainer credentials phished. Malicious versions published for several hours before takedown. Payload hooked crypto wallet provider APIs to redirect transactions.

duckdb_sep_2025.title: DuckDB supply chain compromise
duckdb_sep_2025.notes: Same actor as chalk/debug Sep 2025 campaign. DuckDB npm packages compromised in parallel.

eslint_prettier_phishing_jul_2025.title: ESLint/Prettier phishing compromise
eslint_prettier_phishing_jul_2025.notes: Maintainer phished. Malicious versions published for eslint-config-prettier and related packages. postinstall script exfiltrated npm tokens.

nx_s1ngularity_aug_2025.title: Nx / S1ngularity supply chain
nx_s1ngularity_aug_2025.notes: Multiple @nx/* scoped packages and the nx meta-package compromised across a range of versions. Payload scanned for GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, AWS credentials and posted to attacker webhook.

shai_hulud_worm_v1_sep_2025.title: Shai-Hulud self-propagating npm worm (v1)
shai_hulud_worm_v1_sep_2025.notes: First npm worm with self-propagation via stolen maintainer credentials. @ctrl/tinycolor was the initial vector before the worm was contained.

react_native_mar_2026.title: React Native country/phone picker compromise
react_native_mar_2026.notes: Two popular React Native packages compromised. Payload targeted mobile device credentials and keychain items.

ghost_campaign_feb_2026.title: Ghost campaign (entirely malicious packages)
ghost_campaign_feb_2026.notes: Entirely malicious packages published under ghost accounts. Names designed to look legitimate. All versions malicious.

nk_contagious_interview_wave3_mar_2026.title: North Korean Contagious Interview wave 3
nk_contagious_interview_wave3_mar_2026.notes: Third wave of DPRK-linked Contagious Interview campaign. Packages distributed to software developer targets via fake job interviews. All versions malicious.

credential_harvester_typosquats_2025.title: Credential harvester typosquats
credential_harvester_typosquats_2025.notes: Typosquat campaign targeting Discord, Ethereum, and framework package names. Often use .js suffix as typosquat signal.

nodecordrat_bitcoin_typosquats_2025.title: NodeCordRAT / Bitcoin library typosquats
nodecordrat_bitcoin_typosquats_2025.notes: Typosquats of Bitcoin library names carrying NodeCordRAT payload.

lazarus_graphalgo_may2025_feb2026.title: Lazarus Group / Graphalgo campaign
lazarus_graphalgo_may2025_feb2026.notes: Long-running Lazarus-attributed campaign using math/graph library names.

rat_typosquats_2026.title: RAT-delivering typosquats 2026
rat_typosquats_2026.notes: Typosquat packages delivering remote access trojans.

teampcp_wave1_mar_2026.title: TeamPCP Wave 1: Trivy to Checkmarx Actions compromise
teampcp_wave1_mar_2026.notes: TeamPCP used stolen Trivy CI credentials to compromise Checkmarx GitHub Actions. Payload harvested CI secrets, SSH keys, cloud credentials. Exfil to checkmarx[.]zone.

teampcp_wave3_bitwarden_apr_2026.title: TeamPCP Wave 3: Bitwarden CLI npm worm
teampcp_wave3_bitwarden_apr_2026.notes: Cascading from Checkmarx Actions compromise. @bitwarden/cli 2026.4.0 contained self-propagating npm worm with Bun-based JS loader, GitHub commit dead-drop C2, and credential harvesting targeting AI coding assistant configs (~/.claude.json).

telnyx_wav_steg_apr_2026.title: Telnyx WAV steganography compromise
telnyx_wav_steg_apr_2026.notes: TeamPCP compromised Telnyx PyPI package. Payload hidden in spec-valid WAV audio files (ringtone.wav/hangup.wav). XOR-encrypted executable decoded from audio frames.

mini_shai_hulud_apr_2026.title: Mini Shai-Hulud: SAP CAP framework worm (TeamPCP Wave 6)
mini_shai_hulud_apr_2026.family: self-propagating worm + credential stealer + CI abuse
mini_shai_hulud_apr_2026.notes: TeamPCP Wave 6. Cascading from Bitwarden CLI compromise (Wave 5). Preinstall hook downloads Bun 1.3.13, executes 11.7MB obfuscated JS (PBKDF2+SHA256 cipher, salt 'ctf-scramble-v2'). Steals credentials from 39+ file paths (GitHub, npm, AWS, Azure, GCP, SSH, Kubernetes, Terraform, crypto wallets, FileZilla). Exfiltrates via victim's own GitHub account (creates public repos with description 'A Mini Shai-Hulud has Appeared'). Injects Claude Code SessionStart hooks and VS Code folderOpen tasks for persistence. Installs self-hosted GHA runner named 'SHA1HULUD' with discussion.yaml C2 workflow. SAP CI abused via OIDC token exchange to publish @cap-js packages.

tanstack_shai_hulud_may_2026.title: Mini Shai-Hulud: TanStack Router Worm (TeamPCP Wave 7)
tanstack_shai_hulud_may_2026.family: self-propagating worm + credential stealer + provenance forging
tanstack_shai_hulud_may_2026.notes: CVE-2026-45321 (CVSS 9.6). CI pipeline hijack via pull_request_target + cache poisoning + OIDC memory extraction from runner /proc/pid/mem. 42 TanStack packages, 84 versions in 6-minute window. Payload: 2.3MB obfuscated router_init.js with beautify() AES decryption. Steals AWS, GCP, K8s, Vault, npm, GitHub, SSH, AI configs, browser passwords, crypto wallets. Exfiltrates via Session P2P (getsession.org). Self-propagates by republishing victim npm packages with forged Sigstore/SLSA provenance. Dead-man wiper: find ~ -type f -writable | xargs shred.

tanstack_secondary_victims_may_2026.title: Mini Shai-Hulud: Secondary Victims (Worm Propagation from TanStack)
tanstack_secondary_victims_may_2026.notes: Packages compromised via worm propagation from TanStack. 373+ malicious versions across 169+ packages including @mistralai/mistralai, @opensearch-project/opensearch, 65+ @uipath/* packages. Specific affected versions not yet enumerated here; use --update-vulns for live OSV data. IOC detection via router_init.js payload filename and getsession.org exfil domain provides coverage independent of version pinning.

node_ipc_credential_stealer_may_2026.title: node-ipc credential stealer (May 2026)
node_ipc_credential_stealer_may_2026.notes: IIFE appended to CJS entrypoint, no install hooks, credential harvester, DNS exfil. Dormant maintainer account takeover via expired domain re-registration (Jan 2025 expiry, May 7 2026 re-registration). Socket Research disclosure May 14 2026.

canisterworm_icp_apr_2026.title: CanisterWorm ICP blockchain C2 (Apr 2026)
canisterworm_icp_apr_2026.notes: ICP blockchain C2 at canister cjn37-uyaaa-aaaac-qgnva-cai.raw.icp0.io. No domain seizure possible, no hosting provider takedown. Keepsecure Labs disclosure April 22 2026.

shai_hulud_copycats_may_2026.title: Shai-Hulud open-source copycat packages (May 2026)
shai_hulud_copycats_may_2026.notes: Copycat packages using modified C2, based on open-sourced Shai-Hulud worm code published by TeamPCP on GitHub (MIT license) with $1K bounty contest. May 12 2026.

nx_console_vscode_may_2026.title: VS Code nrwl.angular-console extension compromise (May 2026)
nx_console_vscode_may_2026.family: credential stealer + VS Code extension compromise
nx_console_vscode_may_2026.notes: VS Code extension compromise. Stolen credentials from TanStack @tanstack/zod-adapter infection (7-day dwell time). Targets ~/.claude/settings.json. Led to exfiltration of ~3800 GitHub internal repos. 11-minute window, ~6000 activations via auto-update. StepSecurity disclosure May 18 2026.

antv_worm_propagation_may_2026.title: Mini Shai-Hulud: AntV/ECharts Worm Propagation (May 2026)
antv_worm_propagation_may_2026.family: self-propagating worm + credential stealer + provenance forging
antv_worm_propagation_may_2026.notes: Worm propagation from TanStack compromise. 320+ packages, 639 malicious versions, 59M monthly downloads affected. Preinstall hook installs Bun runtime, writes persistence to .claude/ directories, forges SLSA provenance. May 19 2026.

vpmdhaj_opensearch_typosquats_may_2026.title: vpmdhaj OpenSearch/Elastic typosquat credential-stealer cluster
vpmdhaj_opensearch_typosquats_may_2026.family: typosquat credential stealer + cloud/CI secret harvester
vpmdhaj_opensearch_typosquats_may_2026.notes: Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported 14 npm packages published by alias vpmdhaj, with more than 1700 downloads. Payload used Bun, cloud metadata access, npm token enumeration, Secret Manager enumeration, X-Supply HTTP header, aab.sportsontheweb.net C2, payload.bin, opensearch_init.js/ai_init.js, and __DAEMONIZED=1.

miasma_redhat_cloud_services_jun_2026.title: Miasma / Red Hat Cloud Services supply-chain compromise
miasma_redhat_cloud_services_jun_2026.family: trusted namespace compromise + lifecycle credential stealer + worm propagation
miasma_redhat_cloud_services_jun_2026.notes: Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported 32 maliciously modified @redhat-cloud-services packages across more than 90 versions. The packages carried authentic provenance signatures from a legitimate GitHub Actions OIDC publishing workflow, used npm preinstall, downloaded Bun, harvested GitHub/npm/cloud/Vault/Kubernetes/developer credentials, scraped runner memory, and attempted worm-like republishing with forged Sigstore/SLSA provenance.
